long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: In the reaction, P + Q → R + S the time taken for 75% reaction of P is twice the time taken for 50% reaction of P. The concentration of Q varies with reaction time as shown in the figure. The overall order of the reaction is
questions.solution_summary: t75% = 2t50% indicates first order with respect to P. From graph [Q] is linearly decreasing with time, i.e., order with respect to Q is zero. Overall order = 1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Determination of order from half-life relationship and graph analysis.

questions.question_text: Consider the following complex ions, P = [FeF6]³⁻, Q = [V(H2O)6]²⁺ and R = [Fe(H2O)6]²⁺. The correct order of the complex ions, according to their spin-only magnetic moment values (in B.M.) is
questions.solution_summary: P: Fe³⁺ has 5 unpaired electrons (d⁵ high spin) → μ = √35 = 5.92 BM. Q: V²⁺ has 3 unpaired electrons (d³) → μ = √15 = 3.87 BM. R: Fe²⁺ has 4 unpaired electrons (d⁶ high spin) → μ = √24 = 4.90 BM. Order: Q < R < P.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of spin-only magnetic moments based on d-electron configuration.

questions.question_text: The arrangement of X⁻ ions around A⁺ ion in solid AX is given in the figure (not drawn to scale). If the radius of X⁻ is 250 pm, the radius of A⁺ is
questions.solution_summary: A⁺ is present in octahedral void of X⁻. For octahedral void, r_void = 0.414 × r_sphere. r_A = 0.414 × 250 = 103.5 ≈ 104 pm.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of radius ratio for octahedral void.

questions.question_text: Concentrated nitric acid, upon long standing, turns yellow-brown due to the formation of
questions.solution_summary: 4HNO₃ → 2H₂O + 4NO₂ + O₂. NO₂ remains dissolved in nitric acid colouring it yellow or even red.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of nitric acid decomposition reaction.

questions.question_text: The compound that does NOT liberate CO₂ on treatment with aqueous sodium bicarbonate solution, is
questions.solution_summary: pKₐ of phenol is 9.98 and that of carbonic acid (H₂CO₃) is 6.63. Phenol is weaker acid than carbonic acid, hence does not give effervescence with NaHCO₃.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of acidity of phenol and carbonic acid.

questions.question_text: Sulfide ores are common for the metals
questions.solution_summary: Ag → Argentite (Ag₂S), Pb → Galena (PbS), Cu → Copper glance (Cu₂S). All are sulfide ores.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of sulfide ores of metals.

questions.question_text: Methylene blue, from its aqueous solution, is adsorbed on activated charcoal at 25°C. For this process, the correct statement is
questions.solution_summary: Adsorption of methylene blue on activated charcoal is physical adsorption, characterized by decrease in enthalpy.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of characteristics of physical adsorption.

questions.question_text: KI in acetone, undergoes SN2 reaction with each of P, Q, R and S. The rates of the reaction vary as
questions.solution_summary: Relative reactivity for SN2: primary > secondary > tertiary. Allylic and benzylic halides are more reactive. The order is P > R > Q > S.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of SN2 reaction rates based on substrate structure.

questions.question_text: The standard enthalpies of formation of CO₂(g), H₂O(l) and glucose(s) at 25°C are -400 kJ/mol, -300 kJ/mol and -1300 kJ/mol respectively. The standard enthalpy of combustion per gram of glucose at 25°C is
questions.solution_summary: Combustion of glucose: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O. ΔH_combustion = (6×-400 + 6×-300) - (-1300) = -2900 kJ/mol = -2900/180 = -16.11 kJ/g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of enthalpy of combustion using Hess's law.

questions.question_text: Upon treatment with ammoniacal H₂S, the metal ion that precipitates as a sulfide is
questions.solution_summary: Among Fe³⁺, Al³⁺, Mg²⁺, Zn²⁺ only Zn²⁺ is precipitated with ammoniacal H₂S as ZnS in Group IV.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of qualitative analysis group separation.

questions.question_text: The initial rate of hydrolysis of methyl acetate (1 M) by a weak acid (HA, 1M) is 1/100th of that of a strong acid (HX, 1M), at 25°C. The K_a of HA is
questions.solution_summary: Rate in weak acid = (1/100) rate in strong acid, so [H⁺]_weak = (1/100)[H⁺]_strong = 10⁻² M. For weak acid, K_a = [H⁺]²/C = (10⁻²)²/1 = 10⁻⁴.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Determination of Ka from relative rates of hydrolysis.

questions.question_text: The hyperconjugate stabilities of tert-butyl cation and 2-butene, respectively, are due to
questions.solution_summary: tert-butyl cation is stabilised by σ→p (empty) electron delocalisation from adjacent C-H bonds into the empty p orbital of the carbocation. 2-butene is stabilised by σ→π* electron delocalisation from adjacent C-H bonds into the π* orbital of the double bond.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of hyperconjugation mechanism in carbocation and alkene stabilisation.

questions.question_text: The pair(s) of coordination complexes/ions exhibiting the same kind of isomerism is(are)
questions.solution_summary: [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]⁺ (octahedral) and [Pt(NH₃)₂(H₂O)Cl]⁺ (square planar) show geometrical isomerism. [Pt(NH₃)₃(NO₃)]Cl and [Pt(NH₃)₃Cl]Br show ionisation isomerism.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification and comparison of isomerism types in coordination compounds.

questions.question_text: Among P, Q, R and S, the aromatic compound(s) is/are
questions.solution_summary: All four compounds (P, Q, R, S) are aromatic. P and Q are formed via aromatic pathways. R is formed via cyclisation with NH₃ followed by oxidation. S is formed via acid hydrolysis.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of aromatic compounds based on structure and Huckel rule.

questions.question_text: Benzene and naphthalene form an ideal solution at room temperature. For this process, the true statement(s) is(are)
questions.solution_summary: For ideal solution formation: ΔS_system > 0 (mixing increases entropy), ΔS_surroundings = 0 (no heat exchange with surroundings), ΔH_mixing = 0 (no enthalpy change). ΔG = ΔH - TΔS is negative (spontaneous), not positive.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Thermodynamic understanding of ideal solution formation.

questions.question_text: The atomic masses of He and Ne are 4 and 20 a.m.u. respectively. The value of the de Broglie wavelength of He gas at -73°C is 'M' times that of the de Broglie wavelength of Ne at 727°C. M is

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of de Broglie wavelength and kinetic energy relationship.

questions.question_text: EDTA⁴⁻ is ethylenediaminetetraacetate ion. The total number of N-Co-O bond angles in [Co(EDTA)]⁻ complex ion is
questions.solution_summary: EDTA is a hexadentate ligand forming 5 chelate rings with Co³⁺. The total number of N-Co-O bond angles in the complex is 8.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting N-Co-O bond angles in EDTA complex.

questions.question_text: The total number of carboxylic acid groups in the product P is
questions.solution_summary: The starting compound undergoes oxidative cleavage to give a dicarboxylic acid product with two -COOH groups.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from oxidative cleavage and counting carboxylic acid groups.

questions.question_text: A tetrapeptide has -COOH group on alanine. This produces glycine (Gly), valine (Val), phenyl alanine (Phe) and alanine (Ala), on complete hydrolysis. For this tetrapeptide, the number of possible sequences (primary structures) with -NH₂ group attached to a chiral center is
questions.solution_summary: COOH is on alanine, so alanine is at one terminus (…-Ala). To have -NH₂ attached to a chiral center, the N-terminal amino acid must be chiral. Glycine is achiral. Possible sequences: 3! = 6 total, but 2 have glycine at N-terminus (achiral), so 4 sequences have -NH₂ on chiral center.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting peptide sequences with NH₂ on chiral center.

questions.question_text: The total number of lone-pairs of electrons in melamine is
questions.solution_summary: Melamine (C₃H₆N₆) has three -NH₂ groups attached to a triazine ring. Each nitrogen has one lone pair (3 from amino groups + 3 from ring nitrogens) = 6 lone pairs.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting lone pairs in melamine structure.

questions.question_text: The K_sp of Ag₂CrO₄ is 1.1×10⁻¹² at 298K. The solubility (in mol/L) of Ag₂CrO₄ in a 0.1M AgNO₃ solution is

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculation of solubility in presence of common ion.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction, the product(s) formed is(are)
questions.solution_summary: The reaction proceeds via a specific mechanism giving the product shown in option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from reaction sequence.

questions.question_text: The major product(s) of the following reaction is (are)
questions.solution_summary: The reaction gives the major product shown in option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Major product prediction from reaction sequence.

questions.question_text: After completion of the reactions (I and II), the organic compound(s) in the reaction mixtures is(are)
questions.solution_summary: Using limiting reagent analysis, Reaction I gives T, U, and acetone; Reaction II gives P.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product identification using limiting reagent concept.

questions.question_text: The correct statement(s) about O₃ is(are)
questions.solution_summary: O₃ has equal O-O bond lengths (resonance), is diamagnetic (no unpaired electrons), and has a bent structure (bond angle ~117°). Thermal decomposition of O₃ is exothermic (2O₃ → 3O₂, ΔH = -142 kJ mol⁻¹), not endothermic.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of ozone structure and properties.

questions.question_text: In the nuclear transmutation ⁹₄Be + X → ⁸₄Be + Y, (X,Y) is (are)
questions.solution_summary: ⁹₄Be + γ → ⁸₄Be + ¹₀n (X=γ, Y=n). Also ⁹₄Be + ¹₁p → ⁸₄Be + ²₁H (X=p, Y=D).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of particles in nuclear transmutation.

questions.question_text: The carbon-based reduction method is NOT used for the extraction of
questions.solution_summary: Fe₂O₃ and SnO₂ undergo carbon reduction. Al₂O₃ (electrolytic reduction) and MgCO₃·CaCO₃ (electrolytic or thermal reduction of MgO) are not extracted by carbon reduction.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Knowledge of metallurgical extraction methods.

questions.question_text: The thermal dissociation equilibrium of CaCO₃(s) is studied under different conditions. CaCO₃(s) ⇌ CaO(s) + CO₂(g). For this equilibrium, the correct statement(s) is(are)
questions.solution_summary: ΔH is dependent on T (Kirchhoff's equation). K is independent of initial amount of CaCO₃ (pure solid). K is independent of pressure of CO₂ (K = pCO₂, constant at given T). ΔH is independent of catalyst.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of heterogeneous equilibrium and thermodynamic principles.

questions.question_text: An aqueous solution of a mixture of two inorganic salts, when treated with dilute HCl, gave a precipitate P and a filtrate Q. The precipitate P was found to dissolve in hot water. The filtrate Q remained unchanged, when treated with H₂S in a dilute mineral acid medium. However, it gave a precipitate R with H₂S in an ammoniacal medium. The precipitate R gave a coloured solution S, when treated with H₂O₂ in an aqueous NaOH medium. The precipitate P contains
questions.solution_summary: Pb²⁺ + 2HCl → PbCl₂ (precipitate soluble in hot water). This is Group I separation.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of cation based on qualitative analysis reactions.

questions.question_text: The coloured solution S contains
questions.solution_summary: Cr³⁺ in ammoniacal medium gives Cr(OH)₃ precipitate, which on treatment with H₂O₂ in NaOH gives Na₂CrO₄ (yellow solution).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of coloured solution from qualitative analysis sequence.

questions.question_text: P and Q are isomers of dicarboxylic acid C₄H₄O₄. Both decolorize Br₂/H₂O. On heating, P forms the cyclic anhydride. Upon treatment with dilute alkaline KMnO₄, P as well as Q could produce one or more than one from S, T and U. Compounds formed from P and Q are, respectively
questions.solution_summary: P is maleic acid (cis) which on KMnO₄ gives optically active S. Q is fumaric acid (trans) which gives optically active pair (T, U).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from oxidation of isomeric dicarboxylic acids.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction sequences V and W are, respectively
questions.solution_summary: The reaction sequences give V and W as shown in option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product prediction from multi-step reaction sequence.

questions.question_text: A fixed mass 'm' of a gas is subjected to transformation of states from K to L to M to N and back to K as shown in the figure. The succeeding operations that enable this transformation of states are
questions.solution_summary: K→L: heating (isobaric expansion), L→M: cooling (isochoric pressure decrease), M→N: cooling (isobaric compression), N→K: heating (isochoric pressure increase). So Heating, Cooling, Cooling, Heating.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of thermodynamic processes from PV diagram.

questions.question_text: The pair of isochoric processes among the transformation of states is
questions.solution_summary: Isochoric processes occur at constant volume (vertical lines on PV diagram). L→M and N→K are isochoric processes.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of isochoric processes from PV diagram.

questions.question_text: The reactions of Cl₂ gas with cold-dilute and hot-concentrated NaOH in water give sodium salts of two (different) oxoacids of chlorine, P and Q respectively. P and Q, respectively, are the sodium salts of
questions.solution_summary: 2NaOH + Cl₂ → NaCl + NaClO + H₂O (cold dilute) → P is sodium hypochlorite (hypochlorous acid salt). 6NaOH + 3Cl₂ → 5NaCl + NaClO₃ + 3H₂O (hot conc) → Q is sodium chlorate (chloric acid salt).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of products from chlorine reactions with NaOH.

questions.question_text: The Cl₂ gas reacts with SO₂ gas, in presence of charcoal, to give a product R. R reacts with white phosphorus to give a compound S. On hydrolysis, S gives an oxoacid of phosphorus, T. R, S and T, respectively, are
questions.solution_summary: Cl₂ + SO₂ → SO₂Cl₂ (R). SO₂Cl₂ + P₄ → PCl₅ + SO₂ (S). PCl₅ + 4H₂O → H₃PO₄ + 5HCl (T).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product identification from multi-step reaction sequence.

questions.question_text: The unbalanced chemical reactions given in List-I show missing reagent or condition (?) which are provided in List-II. Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists.
questions.solution_summary: P: PbO₂ + H₂SO₄ → PbSO₄ + H₂O + O₂ (Warm) → (3). Q: Na₂S₂O₃ + Cl₂ + H₂O → NaCl + NaHSO₄ + S (Cl₂) → (4). R: N₂H₄ + I₂ → N₂ + HI (I₂) → (2). S: XeF₂ + 2NO → Xe + 2NOF (NO) → (1). So P→3, Q→4, R→2, S→1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching reactions with appropriate reagents/conditions.

questions.question_text: Match the chemical conversions in List-I with appropriate reagents in List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists.
questions.solution_summary: P: Cl with NaOEt gives substitution (2). Q: ONa with EtBr gives ether formation (3). R: alkene with Hg(OAc)₂/NaBH₄ gives alcohol (1). S: alkene with BH₃/H₂O₂/NaOH gives anti-Markovnikov alcohol (4).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching chemical conversions with appropriate reagents.

questions.question_text: An aqueous solution of X is added slowly to an aqueous solution of Y as shown in List-I. The variation in conductivity of these reactions in List-II. Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists.
questions.solution_summary: P: (C₅H₅N + CH₃COOH) → Conductivity increases and then does not change much (3). Q: (KI + AgNO₃) → Conductivity does not change much and then increases (4). R: (CH₃COOH + KOH) → Conductivity decreases and then does not change much (2). S: (NaOH + HI) → Conductivity decreases and then increases (1).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching titration reactions with conductivity variation patterns.

questions.question_text: Match the electrochemical processes in List-I with the corresponding electrode potentials (E°) in List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists.
questions.solution_summary: Using thermodynamic relationships (ΔG° = -nFE°): P: Cu²⁺/Cu → E° = +0.34 V (1). Q: O₂/OH⁻ → E° = +0.40 V (2). R: Cu⁺/Cu → E° = +0.52 V (3). S: Cr³⁺/Cr → E° = -0.74 V (4).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching electrochemical processes with electrode potentials.
